framework.name: Paper positions OAuth/OIDC/SAML as fundamentally insufficient, not extensible
framework.description: The paper explicitly argues against extending OAuth 2.0 for agent auth, claiming purpose-built protocols are needed. This directly contrasts with IETF's approach of extending OAuth via token-exchange drafts. Signals fragmentation between 'extend existing' (IETF) vs 'build new' (CSA/academic) camps.
framework.raw_quote: We contend that merely adapting existing protocols is insufficient. Instead, a purpose-built approach is required.

source.title: A Novel Zero-Trust Identity Framework for Agentic AI - arXiv
